mission.title: containerd: Error messages returned from registries should hide sensitive information
mission.description: Error messages returned from registries should hide sensitive information. This issue affects 7+ users.

steps.title: Identify containerd troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your containerd deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n containerd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=containerd
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=containerd -n containerd --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for error: `code = Unknown desc = failed to pull and unpack image "my-private-registry.io/default/nginx:latest": failed to copy: htt`

steps.title: Review containerd configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant containerd configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n containerd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=containerd
kubectl get configmap -n containerd -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=containerd
```
**Description**
Error messages returned from registries should hide sensitive information.

Registries sometimes return a body with an error which contains useful information, but also sensitive data.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Error messages returned from registries should hide…
steps.description:
Trying to fix #5453
```yaml
Container Runtime Version:  containerd://1.4.3
```

steps.title: Confirm Error messages returned from registries should… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=containerd -n containerd --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n containerd --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that `code = Unknown desc = failed to pull and unpack image "my-private-registry.io/default/nginx:latest": failed to copy: htt` no longer appears in logs.

resolution.summary: See the fix PR for the community-verified solution: https://github.com/containerd/containerd/pull/12491

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with containerd installed or the issue environment reproducible.
